context.project_description: Build token usage tracking for LLM queries across all provider integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to enable cost visibility and performance optimization by recording input/output tokens per query.
key_requirements: Add token counting infrastructure that integrates with existing provider protocols
Track token usage at the query level for chat completion and embedding calls
Maintain backward compatibility without modifying core LLM APIs
constraints: Must integrate with existing ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, and other protocol definitions in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Cannot modify or break existing public APIs that clients depend on
Instrumentation must work across all current and future LLM provider implementations
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py provides the hook system with BenchmarkHook protocol, InstrumentedProxy class for method wrapping; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider protocols that all concrete providers must implement; fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py contain provider implementations that need token tracking integration

in_scope: Token tracking on chat completion calls (`chat()` methods)
Instrumentation infrastructure that works across providers
Basic token counting per call context

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Core infrastructure for hooks and wrapping
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
fitz_ai/llm/client.py — The gateway through which providers are accessed
fitz_ai/llm/factory.py — Factory functions for creating providers
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_tracker.py — Contains token counting logic for each provider type and implementation of benchmark hook that records input/output tokens
fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/provider_wrappers.py — Wrapping factory functions to automatically instrument providers and adapters for different provider-specific token counting
fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/__init__.py — Exports key components for easy import into LLM subsystems

assumptions.assumption: Token counting is based on actual API calls rather than estimate
assumptions.impact: Would need to implement approximate estimations using heuristics (but not ideal)

assumptions.assumption: Instrumentation happens at method call level
assumptions.impact: Might need a different approach like context managers or middleware

assumptions.assumption: No streaming token counting is required for MVP
assumptions.impact: Would need to modify `chat_stream` tracking separately

assumptions.assumption: Providers that don't expose token counts natively will use approximations
assumptions.impact: Would need to provide more sophisticated native counting where possible

assumptions.assumption: Cost calculation is out of scope
assumptions.impact: Would need to include provider-specific price models in the tracking logic

assumptions.assumption: Only standard completion/encoding methods are tracked
assumptions.impact: Might want to include more granular tracking (e.g., `rerank`)

assumptions.assumption: Existing provider factory functions are used as entry points
assumptions.impact: Would have to modify how providers are instantiated

approaches.name: Approach A (Corrected)
approaches.description: Enhanced Provider Wrappers with Raw Response Capture using existing instrumentation infrastructure
pros: Integrates with existing Instrumentation system (BenchmarkHook, InstrumentedProxy)
Preserves all provider contracts without breaking changes
Handles varying response structures per-provider through specific parsing logic
Maintains backward compatibility required by client APIs
cons: Requires modification of how providers are instantiated since factory file doesn't exist
Necessitates capturing raw API responses before type conversion to application-level types
Streaming support requires chunk-level token accumulation logic in hook implementation
Context propagation needs additional global context extraction mechanisms
best_for: Production systems requiring token tracking without breaking existing APIs

approaches.name: Approach B (Middleware Layer)
approaches.description: Introduces a middleware layer that intercepts LLM calls before they reach providers
pros: Abstracts provider-specific differences at a higher level
cons: Doesn't exist in current architecture and would break established patterns where direct get_chat() calls are used
Introduces additional complexity with new integration points
Incompatible with this codebase's design philosophy of explicit, maintainable interfaces
best_for: Architectures that support layered request/response processing pipelines

architecture.reasoning: This approach is right because it integrates with existing instrumentation infrastructure and preserves all provider contracts without breaking changes. It handles varying response structures through specific parsing logic for each provider type while maintaining backward compatibility as required by client APIs. The other approaches are wrong because Approach B introduces middleware that doesn't exist in the current architecture and breaks established patterns, making it incompatible with direct get_chat() usage. Approach C (monkey-patching) creates brittle integration points that conflict with this codebase's explicit interface design philosophy.

key_tradeoffs.raw_response_capture_vs_structured_retur: Capturing raw API responses instead of relying on structured returns allows for token extraction from varied response formats, but requires more complex wrapper logic and breaks the assumption that provider methods return processed data.
key_tradeoffs.provider_specific_parsing_vs_unified_int: Implementing provider-specific parsing logic handles diverse response schemas (OpenAI usage fields, Cohere meta.tokens, Anthropic usage object) at cost of increased complexity compared to a unified interface approach.
key_tradeoffs.streaming_support_overhead_vs_accuracy: Adding streaming token accumulation across chunks improves accuracy but introduces overhead and complexity in tracking stateful responses during ongoing streams.

technology_considerations: OpenAI SDK for handling usage fields in response objects
Cohere API client to parse meta.tokens from response metadata
Anthropic SDK with usage object parsing capability
Ollama API integration requiring custom token tracking due to lack of built-in usage reporting
InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook framework for non-intrusive instrumentation
HTTP-level response capture mechanism before type conversion in provider wrappers
architecture.scope_statement: Produces three files: token_tracker.py for benchmark hook implementation, provider_wrappers.py for factory functions to wrap providers, and requires modifications to existing provider instantiation logic in client code paths to support raw response capture before type conversion.

adrs.title: ADR: Enhanced Token Tracking via Provider Wrappers
adrs.context: The original token usage tracking system had critical architectural flaws due to mismatches between expected API behaviors and actual provider contracts, lack of core infrastructure files, and unaddressed edge cases like streaming support.
adrs.decision: Adopt a revised approach using enhanced provider wrappers with raw response capture that integrates with existing instrumentation infrastructure without breaking changes.
adrs.rationale: This approach preserves all existing provider contracts while enabling token tracking by intercepting raw API responses before type conversion. It leverages the established benchmark hook system and maintains backward compatibility required by client APIs.
consequences: All LLM providers must be wrapped with new instrumentation logic to capture raw responses
Provider-specific parsing logic is needed for different response formats (OpenAI, Cohere, Anthropic, Ollama)
Streaming support requires chunk-level token accumulation in the hook implementation
Integration points need modification in client code paths where providers are instantiated
Requires capturing HTTP responses before string/list conversion occurs
alternatives_considered: Approach B (Middleware Layer) - rejected because it introduces a middleware layer that doesn't exist in current architecture and breaks established patterns of direct get_chat() calls
Approach C (Monkey-patching) - rejected because it creates brittle integration points incompatible with this codebase's design philosophy of explicit, maintainable interfaces

adrs.title: ADR: Centralized Token Tracking with Provider-Specific Parsing
adrs.context: The architecture uses a centralized TokenTrackingHook that handles varying response structures from different providers through specific parsing logic while maintaining backward compatibility.
adrs.decision: Accept a tradeoff of increased coupling between the hook and provider implementations by centralizing parsing logic rather than using a more distributed strategy.
adrs.rationale: This approach preserves all existing contracts without breaking changes, which is critical for client API stability. The centralized parsing ensures consistent handling of token state transitions across providers while maintaining backward compatibility in the public interface.
consequences: The TokenTrackingHook becomes tightly coupled to specific provider response formats and parsing logic
Adding new providers requires modifying existing parsing logic rather than creating entirely new handlers
Failure in one provider's parsing logic could potentially affect token tracking for other providers
alternatives_considered: Distributed approach where each provider implements its own token tracking logic with a common interface - would reduce coupling but require more complex orchestration and potential breaking changes to existing contracts

Event-driven architecture that emits provider-specific events rather than direct parsing - would provide better decoupling but introduce additional complexity in maintaining state consistency across distributed components

components.name: TokenTrackingHook
components.purpose: Implements benchmark hook that extracts usage from raw responses
responsibilities: Capture metadata for tracking token usage
Extract tokens from captured raw API responses
Log usage data for different provider types
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: TokenTrackerFactory
components.purpose: Provides factory functions to wrap providers with token tracking capabilities
responsibilities: Wrap OpenAIChat provider with instrumentation for token tracking
Wrap AnthropicChat provider with instrumentation for token tracking
Wrap CohereChat provider with instrumentation for token tracking

ExternalSystem — Anthropic API (chat, vision) via fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py

adrs.context: The original token usage tracking system had critical architectural flaws due to mismatches between expected API behaviors and actual provider contracts, lack of core infrastructure files, and unaddressed edge cases like streaming support.

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Minimal Token Tracking Implementation
phases.objective: Create a single-purpose hook to extract token usage from LLM responses using existing instrumentation infrastructure
deliverables: TokenTrackingHook class in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py implementing BenchmarkHook protocol with token counting logic
key_risks: Response parsing logic correctness for each provider
Missing or malformed 'usage' keys in API responses
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import TokenTrackingHook; h = TokenTrackingHook(); print('Hook created successfully')"

phases.name: Integrate Tracking Into Provider Calls
phases.objective: Modify provider factories or wrapping logic to conditionally register token tracking hook around LLM method calls
deliverables: Instrumentation integration in fitz_ai/llm/client.py or provider factory logic
key_risks: Hook not being registered early enough
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.client import get_chat; chat = get_chat('openai'); print(type(chat).__name__)"

phases.name: Expose Token Counts
phases.objective: Provide a simple way to access aggregated token usage data from active sessions
deliverables: get_token_usage() function exposed in LLM public API
key_risks: Invalid or corrupted usage data returned by LLMs
Missing token fields due to rate limiting / errors
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.instrumentation import get_token_usage; print('Token usage function accessible')"

risks.description: Each LLM provider returns different response formats (e.g., OpenAI's 'usage', Cohere's metadata, Anthropic's usage object) which could lead to incorrect token parsing if not handled properly for each provider type
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific parsing logic with fallbacks and comprehensive unit testing covering all supported LLM providers' response structures
risks.contingency: If a parsing method fails or is missing, token tracking should silently skip that call rather than crash the system
risks.verification: Write unit tests that mock API responses with known tokens from OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere and Ollama to ensure accurate extraction

risks.description: Instrumentation hooks registration timing where hook might not be registered before provider creation calls are made, causing the hook to not get called for any queries
risks.mitigation: Ensure instrumentation is set up during startup phase and before any LLM client instantiation occurs; add integration test that creates a chat client with instrumented hook present
risks.contingency: If instrumentation fails to register, fall back to manual token tracking in critical paths or disable telemetry but continue normal operation
risks.verification: Add integration test that verifies instrumented provider correctly forwards method calls and records token usage

risks.description: Provider-specific attribute access during response parsing could break if API responses change or have unexpected structures, causing crashes or incomplete token accounting
risks.mitigation: Test each wrapped provider with real mock responses using specific interfaces exposed and implement defensive programming practices like isinstance checks and default fallbacks
risks.contingency: Implement error handling around parsing logic so that malformed or missing fields don't cause application crashes but still log warnings
risks.verification: Test each wrapped provider with various mock API responses including edge cases such as missing usage fields or unexpected data types

risks.description: Missing usage fields in API responses (e.g., error cases) could cause parser to crash if not handled gracefully, leading to silent failures in token tracking
risks.mitigation: Implement defensive parsing that does not crash when 'usage' field is missing and logs warnings instead of throwing exceptions
risks.contingency: Add fallback logic for scenarios where no usage data exists, such as in error responses or when rate limiting occurs
risks.verification: Write unit tests covering edge cases like empty response dictionaries and missing key fields to ensure silent failure mode works correctly

risks.description: Token count overflow or underflow if API returns negative or extremely large numbers which could corrupt reporting metrics
risks.mitigation: Sanitize parsed token values by ensuring they are non-negative integers within reasonable ranges before storing them
risks.contingency: Implement assertions and validation to catch invalid token counts early and log appropriate warnings for investigation
risks.verification: Test with extreme value inputs (negative, very large numbers) and assert that parsed tokens are within expected bounds

recommended_contingencies: If a parsing method fails or is missing, token tracking should silently skip that call rather than crash the system
Implement error handling around parsing logic so that malformed or missing fields don't cause application crashes but still log warnings
Add fallback logic for scenarios where no usage data exists, such as in error responses or when rate limiting occurs
